The Mankin modified by Carlson semi-quantitative histological score $ ^{30} $ was used for chondropathy evaluation of each femoral condyle (medial and lateral) and tibial plateau (medial and lateral)

Finally, to evaluate the static histomorphometric parameters of epiphyseal trabecular bone at a magnification of 20×, two consecutive regions of interest (ROIs) of the same area (2,217,224 μm²) were s
-BV/TV: was calculated as  $ [(B.Ar/T.Ar) \times (4/\pi) \times 100] $, where B.Ar represents the trabecular bone area and T.Ar the ROI area;
-Tb.Th: was calculated as  $ [(2 \times \mathrm{B.Ar})/(\mathrm{B.Pm} \times 4/\pi)] $, where B.Pm is the trabecular bone perimeter;
-Tb.N: represents the number of the trabeculae per unit area and was calculated as  $ [(BV/TV \times 10)/Tb.Th] $;
-Tb.Sp: was calculated as  $ [(1,000/\text{TbN}) - \text{Tb.Th}] $.
The area and perimeter values derived from the binarization process of the imaging analysis system inside the ROIs.
The inter-tester coefficients for these histomorphometric parameters were 0.84 for BV/TV, 0.82 for Tb.Th, 0.78 for Tb.N and 0.79 for Tb.Sp as also performed by Chiba et al. $ ^{33} $

CONCLUSIONS

Although the use of different frequencies should not affect patient compliance to therapy, it was interesting to verify if a lower frequency may eventually reproduce the same biological effects of the

PEMFs are a local treatment and cannot be expected to treat OA in all joints. Nevertheless, OA located at specific joints, most frequently knee and hip, has a severe impact on patients' lives and requ
